rationale: Sarantopoulos et al. (2019) is cited repeatedly in the literature review and Table 1 as a key prior exception on inspirational retail interventions, not just as a background list citation. The citing paper uses it to define a specific research gap (no direct comparison with deal-oriented content) and to motivate boundary-condition theorizing about goal specificity and involvement. The authors also position their contribution as an operational extension: using inspirational communication content instead of physical complement-based assortment reorganization, with added mechanism tests.
relation_description: The citing paper extends Sarantopoulos et al. (2019) by shifting from complement-based assortment organization to inspirational in-store communication as the intervention while examining spending outcomes. It explicitly builds from the prior paper's visualization and goal-specificity/involvement insights to test goal-completion mediation, focal and substitute purchasing, and comparisons against deal-oriented content.
